---
title: "Mary Elson"
about:
  template: jolla
  image: images/IMG_4725.jpeg
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/melson314
---

*Postdoctoral Associate in Behavioral Evolutionary Neuroscience*

Cornell University

I am broadly interested in how ultimate and proximate mechanisms shape acoustic communication across different species. During my PhD I examined how communication during social interactions shapes preferences in the monogamous zebra finch. For many species courtship is approached using a linear signal-receiver model of communication that tends to omit the dynamic nature of these interactions. My work integrates three different levels of analysis -- behavioral interaction, neurobiology, and communication theory -- to ask questions about pair bond and preference formation and extend the systems model of communication.

## Education

PhD, Cornell University | 2025

MA, Cornell University | 2021

MSc, University of Edinburgh | 2018

BS & BA, University of Minnesota | 2016

## Published

**Elson, M. R.**, Hsu, R. K., Ophir, A. G., & Prior, N. H. (2026). Beyond Song—An Investigation of Song and Social Preferences in a Monogamous Songbird. *Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences*, *1557*(1), e70216. https://doi.org/10.1111/nyas.70216

Prior, N. H., Fishbein, A. R., Garcia, E. M., Clough, S., **Elson, M. R.**, Ball, G. F., & Dooling, R. J. (2025). Assessing female call responses to syllable level details in song. *Frontiers in Psychology*, *16*. https://doi.org/10.3389/fpsyg.2025.1523105

**Elson, M. R.**, Prior, N. H., & Ophir, A. G. (2024). Neuroendocrine Mechanisms Underlying Reproductive Decision Making Across Taxa. In H. K. Caldwell & H. E. Albers (Eds.), *Neuroendocrinology of Behavior and Emotions: Environmental and Social Factors Affecting Behavior* (pp. 157–191). Springer International Publishing. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-031-51112-7_7

Faust, K. M., Carouso-Peck, S., **Elson, M. R.**, & Goldstein, M. H. (2020). The Origins of Social Knowledge in Altricial Species. *Annual Review of Developmental Psychology*, *2*(1), null. https://doi.org/10.1146/annurev-devpsych-051820-121446


## In Progress

**Elson MR**, Ball GF, & Prior NH. Context and dopamine dependent effects on female vocal behavior in a monogamous songbird. In prep.

Rivera GL^+^, **Elson MR^+^**, Prior NH. Wooing by example – early social experience shapes female calling during courtship interactions. In prep.

**Elson MR**, Ophir AG, & Prior NH. She loves me, she loves me not – how internal state and social context drive female preferences. In prep.
